clauses.label: Confidentiality Scope
clauses.rationale: Broad scope favors disclosing party. Standard language. From the receiving party perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Confidentiality Scope (buyer position): Recipient shall hold in confidence all information disclosed by Disclosing Party, whether or not marked Confidential, that a reasonable person would deem confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.

clauses.label: Term & Duration
clauses.rationale: 5-year survival is standard for commercial NDAs. From the receiving party perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Term & Duration (buyer position): Confidentiality obligations shall survive for five (5) years following termination of this Agreement.

clauses.label: Return of Materials
clauses.rationale: 30-day window is standard. Consider adding a certification requirement. From the receiving party perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Return of Materials (buyer position): Upon termination, Recipient shall return or destroy all Confidential Materials within thirty (30) days and certify destruction in writing.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.rationale: California is an acceptable jurisdiction for commercial agreements. From the receiving party perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Governing Law (buyer position): This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles.

clauses.label: Remedies
clauses.rationale: Standard injunctive relief provision. From the receiving party perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Remedies (buyer position): Disclosing Party may seek injunctive relief without posting bond for actual or threatened breach of confidentiality obligations.

clauses.label: Confidentiality Scope
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Confidentiality Scope: Broad scope favors disclosing party. Standard language. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for NDA agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Confidentiality Scope: Confidential Information includes all written, oral, and electronic information disclosed in connection with the Purpose, except information that is publicly available through no fault of the receiving party.

clauses.label: Term & Duration
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Term & Duration: 5-year survival is standard for commercial NDAs. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for NDA agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Term & Duration: The term of this Agreement is two (2) years; confidentiality obligations survive for the longer of five (5) years or until information enters the public domain.

clauses.label: Return of Materials
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Return of Materials: 30-day window is standard. Consider adding a certification requirement. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for NDA agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Return of Materials: Recipient may retain one archival copy solely for compliance purposes, subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Governing Law: California is an acceptable jurisdiction for commercial agreements. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for NDA agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Governing Law: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York; parties consent to exclusive jurisdiction in New York County courts.

clauses.label: Remedies

clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Remedies: Remedies are cumulative; failure to exercise a remedy shall not constitute a waiver of future enforcement.

mutual.notice: Negotiation guide for Standard NDA — mutual (both parties) perspective. Positions reflect mock fixture data for demo purposes only; attorney review required before reliance.

clauses.label: Confidentiality Scope
clauses.rationale: The marked-materials-only scope leaves oral disclosures and unmarked email attachments unprotected. As receiving party you bear the risk of inadvertent disclosure without reciprocal protection for information you share.
clauses.fallback_text: Confidential Information shall mean all non-public information disclosed in connection with the Purpose, whether oral, written, or electronic, that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure. The marking requirement in Section 2.1 is deleted.

clauses.label: Term & Duration
clauses.rationale: Three-year survival is below the five-year commercial standard and may not cover the full evaluation cycle if a transaction closes and integration continues.
clauses.fallback_text: Confidentiality obligations shall survive for five (5) years following termination of this Agreement; trade secrets shall remain protected for so long as they qualify as trade secrets under applicable law.

clauses.label: Return of Materials
clauses.rationale: 60-day window is longer than standard 30 days. From the receiving party perspective, acceptable with targeted improvements.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Return of Materials (buyer position): Upon termination, Recipient shall return or destroy all Confidential Materials within thirty (30) days and certify destruction in writing.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.rationale: California jurisdiction acceptable. From the receiving party perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Governing Law (buyer position): This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles.

clauses.label: Indemnification
clauses.rationale: Uncapped indemnification for all losses — including consequential damages and third-party claims — is well outside market for a preliminary NDA. Cal. Civ. Code § 1668 may limit enforceability, but litigation cost is still unacceptable.
clauses.fallback_text: Each party's indemnification obligation shall be limited to direct damages arising from the indemnifying party's unauthorized disclosure of the other party's Confidential Information, capped at the greater of $250,000 or fees paid under any related services agreement in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim. Consequential, punitive, and lost-profit damages are excluded.

clauses.label: Confidentiality Scope
clauses.rationale: The marked-materials requirement protects you as disclosing party — you control what triggers confidentiality obligations and reduce ambiguity over oral pitch content.

clauses.label: Term & Duration

clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Term & Duration (seller position): Confidentiality obligations shall survive for three (3) years following termination, except trade secrets which survive indefinitely.

clauses.rationale: Return of Materials is acceptable as drafted for Client Proposed Non-Disclosure Agreement. 60-day window is longer than standard 30 days.

clauses.label: Indemnification
clauses.rationale: Broad indemnification is your primary remedy if the recipient misuses technical specs or pricing. Resist caps that eliminate recovery for willful misconduct or competitor-directed leaks.
clauses.fallback_text: Indemnification cap shall not apply to breaches caused by Recipient's gross negligence, willful misconduct, or disclosure to a direct competitor. Recipient shall maintain cyber liability insurance naming Disclosing Party as additional insured.

clauses.label: Confidentiality Scope
clauses.rationale: Mutual exploratory discussions require symmetric scope — neither side should rely on marking formalities when engineers exchange architecture diagrams verbally.
clauses.fallback_text: Each party shall protect the other's Confidential Information using at least the same degree of care it uses for its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care. clauses.label: Term & Duration
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Term & Duration: 3-year survival is below the 5-year market standard. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for NDA agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Term & Duration: The term of this Agreement is two (2) years; confidentiality obligations survive for the longer of five (5) years or until information enters the public domain.

clauses.label: Return of Materials
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Return of Materials: 60-day window is longer than standard 30 days. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for NDA agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Return of Materials: Recipient may retain one archival copy solely for compliance purposes, subject to ongoing confidentiality obligations.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Governing Law: California jurisdiction acceptable. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for NDA agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Governing Law: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York; parties consent to exclusive jurisdiction in New York County courts.

clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Data Security: Provider shall not use Client data to train models or improve services without Client's prior written consent; subprocessors require equivalent obligations.

clauses.label: Limitation of Liability

clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Limitation of Liability: Liability cap does not apply to breaches of confidentiality, data security obligations, or either party's indemnification obligations for third-party IP claims.

mutual.notice: Negotiation guide for Tech MSA — mutual (both parties) perspective. Positions reflect mock fixture data for demo purposes only; attorney review required before reliance.

clauses.label: Indemnification
clauses.rationale: Client indemnifies Provider for all third-party claims including data uploaded by Client — without cap. You assume Provider's product defects, security failures, and IP infringement risk.

clauses.label: Limitation of Liability
clauses.rationale: Three-month fee cap is far below enterprise SaaS market (typically 12 months fees or $1M). Provider retains broad consequential damage disclaimer with no reciprocal cap on Client.
clauses.fallback_text: Each party's aggregate liability shall not exceed the greater of fees paid in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim or $2,000,000, except for breaches of confidentiality, data security, or either party's IP indemnification obligations.

clauses.label: Intellectual Property
clauses.rationale: You receive only a term-limited non-exclusive license; custom workflows, integrations, and training data exports may be lost at renewal or termination.
clauses.fallback_text: Client owns all custom configurations, integrations, and reports developed specifically for Client under paid SOWs. Provider grants a perpetual, royalty-free license to embedded Background IP in deliverables. Client data and anonymization rights addressed in a separate data processing addendum.

clauses.label: Data Security
clauses.rationale: 'Commercially reasonable' is vague; training use on client data is implied. From the client perspective, acceptable with targeted improvements.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Data Security (buyer position): Provider shall maintain SOC 2 Type II controls, encrypt data in transit and at rest, and notify Client within twenty-four (24) hours of a confirmed security incident.

clauses.label: Termination
clauses.rationale: Provider may terminate for convenience on 30 days notice while Client may terminate only for uncured material breach — leaving you without exit during a failed implementation or post-acquisition integration.
clauses.fallback_text: Either party may terminate for convenience on ninety (90) days written notice. Client may terminate immediately for Provider's failure to cure a material SLA breach within thirty (30) days. Upon termination, Provider shall export Client data in standard JSON/CSV within thirty (30) days at no additional fee.

clauses.label: Severance Payment
clauses.rationale: Six months base salary without COBRA subsidy, bonus pro-ration, or acceleration of unvested equity undervalues a VP-level separation after five years of service.
clauses.fallback_text: Severance increased to nine (9) months base salary plus target bonus pro-rata for year of separation, COBRA premium reimbursement for nine (9) months, and acceleration of 25% of unvested RSUs scheduled to vest within twelve (12) months of Separation Date.

clauses.label: Non-Compete
clauses.rationale: California Business & Professions Code § 16600 renders most non-competes void. A 50-mile, 12-month restriction creates false comfort for Employer and may taint severance enforceability.
clauses.fallback_text: Non-compete deleted. Replaced with twelve (12) month non-solicitation of employees with whom Employee had material contact, and non-disclosure of trade secrets under Cal. Civ. Code § 3426.

clauses.label: IP Assignment
clauses.rationale: Post-separation improvements sweep captures personal projects and open-source contributions unrelated to Employer business.
clauses.fallback_text: Assignment limited to inventions made during employment using Employer resources or relating to Employer's Confidential Information. Employee's Prior Inventions Schedule (Exhibit A) lists excluded projects; post-separation improvements require substantial use of Employer trade secrets.

clauses.label: Release of Claims
clauses.rationale: ADEA compliance requires specific language and timing; verify OWBPA checklist. From the employee perspective, acceptable with targeted improvements.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Release of Claims (buyer position): Employee releases all claims through Separation Date under federal and state law, excluding workers' compensation and vested benefits.

clauses.label: Confidentiality
clauses.rationale: Standard post-employment confidentiality. From the employee perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Confidentiality (buyer position): Recipient shall hold in confidence all information disclosed by Disclosing Party, whether or not marked Confidential, that a reasonable person would deem confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.

buyer.notice: Negotiation guide for Employment Severance — buyer (employee) perspective. Positions reflect mock fixture data for demo purposes only; attorney review required before reliance.

clauses.label: Severance Payment
clauses.rationale: Severance Payment is acceptable as drafted for Executive Severance and Release Agreement (Draft). Verify COBRA and tax gross-up treatment; six months may be below market for executives.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Severance Payment (seller position): Severance equals six (6) months salary payable in installments aligned with payroll schedule, less applicable withholdings.

clauses.label: Non-Compete
clauses.rationale: Even where partially enforceable, non-compete deters competitive hires and protects customer relationships in multi-state footprint outside California.
clauses.fallback_text: Non-compete enforceable only in jurisdictions permitting restraints; in California, provision replaced by enhanced non-solicitation and garden-leave consulting fee during restricted period.

clauses.label: IP Assignment
clauses.rationale: As employer, IP Assignment strongly protects your position. Post-separation IP sweep is broad; confirm prior inventions schedule. Resist buyer-side revisions that cap remedies or narrow scope.
clauses.fallback_text: Preserve IP Assignment as drafted. If counterparty insists on changes, offer: Assignment covers work product created using Employer resources or relating to Employer's Confidential Information during employment.

clauses.label: Release of Claims
clauses.rationale: Broad release with ADEA waiver is consideration for severance — ensure OWBPA timing and group termination disclosures if applicable.
clauses.fallback_text: Release includes ADEA waiver with twenty-one (21) day consideration period, seven (7) day revocation, and attachment of workforce demographic data if part of group termination program.

clauses.label: Confidentiality
clauses.rationale: Confidentiality is acceptable as drafted for Executive Severance and Release Agreement (Draft). Standard post-employment confidentiality.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Confidentiality (seller position): Each party shall protect the other's Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses for its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care.

seller.notice: Negotiation guide for Employment Severance — seller (employer) perspective. Positions reflect mock fixture data for demo purposes only; attorney review required before reliance.

clauses.label: Severance Payment
clauses.rationale: Balanced separation packages trade enhanced cash for narrower restrictive covenants and mutual non-disparagement — reduces litigation risk for both sides.
clauses.fallback_text: Seven (7) months severance, mutual non-disparagement, neutral reference protocol, and Employer-paid outplacement up to $15,000. Restrictive covenants limited to non-solicit and confidentiality.

clauses.label: Non-Compete
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Non-Compete: Enforceability varies by state; California generally voids non-competes post-2024. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Employment agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Non-Compete: Restrictive covenants enforceable only to the extent permitted by applicable law; if overbroad, courts may blue-pencil to minimum necessary scope.

clauses.label: IP Assignment
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on IP Assignment: Post-separation IP sweep is broad; confirm prior inventions schedule. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Employment agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to IP Assignment: Employee shall assist in securing IP rights at Employer's expense during and after employment for assigned inventions.

clauses.label: Release of Claims
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Release of Claims: ADEA compliance requires specific language and timing; verify OWBPA checklist. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Employment agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Release of Claims: Employee does not release claims arising after signing or rights that cannot be waived as a matter of law.

clauses.label: Confidentiality
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Confidentiality: Standard post-employment confidentiality. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Employment agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Confidentiality: Confidential Information includes all written, oral, and electronic information disclosed in connection with the Purpose, except information that is publicly available through no fault of the receiving party.

mutual.notice: Negotiation guide for Employment Severance — mutual (both parties) perspective. Positions reflect mock fixture data for demo purposes only; attorney review required before reliance.

clauses.label: Purchase Price Adjustment
clauses.rationale: Verify appraisal contingency and credit allocation against LOI. From the buyer perspective, acceptable with targeted improvements.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Purchase Price Adjustment (buyer position): Purchase price adjusted to $12,600,000 with seller credit of $150,000 for deferred maintenance items identified in inspection report.

clauses.label: Due Diligence Extension
clauses.rationale: Extension is reasonable for environmental and structural items. From the buyer perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Due Diligence Extension (buyer position): Inspection period extended ten (10) days for completion of Phase I ESA and structural engineer report at buyer's expense.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.rationale: Texas law consistent with property location. From the buyer perspective, acceptable as drafted.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Governing Law (buyer position): This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles.

clauses.label: Dispute Resolution
clauses.rationale: Mandatory Travis County litigation without arbitration or mediation increases cost if Seller is out-of-state LP with counsel in Dallas only.
clauses.fallback_text: Parties shall mediate in Austin under CPR rules before filing suit. Exclusive jurisdiction remains Travis County; each party consents to personal jurisdiction and waives inconvenient forum only after failed mediation.

clauses.label: As-Is Condition
clauses.rationale: Strict AS-IS with only Schedule B disclosures shifts unknown latent defect risk to Buyer after substantial price increase — inconsistent with extended diligence for roof and Phase I ESA.
clauses.fallback_text: Seller represents no known material defects except those listed in Schedule B as updated through inspection. Seller responsible for remediation of pre-existing environmental conditions identified in Phase I ESA before closing, up to $200,000 escrow holdback.

buyer.notice: Negotiation guide for RE Purchase Amendment — buyer (buyer) perspective. Positions reflect mock fixture data for demo purposes only; attorney review required before reliance.

clauses.label: Purchase Price Adjustment
clauses.rationale: Price increase reflects appraisal gap and TI credits already negotiated — resist further credits unless Buyer proves material adverse inspection items.
clauses.fallback_text: Price firm at $12,750,000; Buyer credit capped at $100,000 for roof items documented in structural report; all other inspection findings deemed waived unless raised before diligence deadline.

clauses.label: Due Diligence Extension
clauses.rationale: Due Diligence Extension is acceptable as drafted for Commercial Purchase Agreement — First Amendment. Extension is reasonable for environmental and structural items.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Due Diligence Extension (seller position): Buyer may terminate during inspection period for any reason with return of earnest money; seller to provide access and existing reports.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.rationale: Governing Law is acceptable as drafted for Commercial Purchase Agreement — First Amendment. Texas law consistent with property location.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Governing Law (seller position): This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California, excluding its conflict-of-laws rules.

clauses.label: Dispute Resolution
clauses.rationale: Dispute Resolution is acceptable as drafted for Commercial Purchase Agreement — First Amendment. No arbitration; buyer loses forum flexibility if seller is out-of-state.
clauses.fallback_text: Proposed revision to Dispute Resolution (seller position): Disputes shall be resolved by litigation in state or federal courts of competent jurisdiction in the county of the defendant's principal place of business.

clauses.label: As-Is Condition
clauses.rationale: AS-IS protects Seller from post-closing repair claims after Buyer had extended inspection — standard in Texas commercial closings.
clauses.fallback_text: AS-IS language preserved; Seller adds disclosure of 2019 roof patch and prior tenant HVAC maintenance logs without assuming repair obligation.

seller.notice: Negotiation guide for RE Purchase Amendment — seller (seller) perspective. Positions reflect mock fixture data for demo purposes only; attorney review required before reliance.

clauses.label: Purchase Price Adjustment
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Purchase Price Adjustment: Verify appraisal contingency and credit allocation against LOI. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Real Estate agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Purchase Price Adjustment: Final price subject to proration of taxes, rent, and CAM charges as of closing date per standard closing statement.

clauses.label: Due Diligence Extension
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Due Diligence Extension: Extension is reasonable for environmental and structural items. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Real Estate agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Due Diligence Extension: Due diligence items limited to environmental, title, and survey; buyer waives further inspection rights after expiration.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Governing Law: Texas law consistent with property location. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Real Estate agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Governing Law: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York; parties consent to exclusive jurisdiction in New York County courts.

clauses.label: Dispute Resolution
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on Dispute Resolution: No arbitration; buyer loses forum flexibility if seller is out-of-state. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Real Estate agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to Dispute Resolution: Parties agree to mediate in good faith before arbitration; prevailing party entitled to reasonable attorneys' fees for IP and confidentiality claims.

clauses.label: As-Is Condition
clauses.rationale: Balanced posture on As-Is Condition: Broad as-is language shifts repair risk to buyer post-closing. Propose symmetric obligations, mutual caps, and notice procedures consistent with market standards for Real Estate agreements.
clauses.fallback_text: Propose mutual revision to As-Is Condition: As-is sale with seller responsible for remediation of pre-closing environmental violations identified in Phase I ESA.

mutual.notice: Negotiation guide for RE Purchase Amendment — mutual (both parties) perspective. Positions reflect mock fixture data for demo purposes only; attorney review required before reliance.
